summary: Completed testing for granular role-based access control (RBAC) feature. All 22 backend API tests passed and all 7 frontend test scenarios passed. The permissions system correctly restricts access for Admin, Editor, and Viewer roles.
passed_tests: P0: Admin login returns user with role=admin
P0: Editor login returns permissions array in user object
P0: Viewer login returns permissions array in user object
P0: GET /api/auth/me returns permissions for editor
P0: GET /api/auth/me returns permissions for viewer
P0: Editor WITH 'news' permission can POST /api/news (200)
P0: Editor WITH 'events' permission can POST /api/events (200)
P0: Editor WITHOUT 'employees' permission gets 403 on POST /api/employees
P0: Editor WITHOUT 'gallery' permission gets 403 on POST /api/albums
P0: Viewer gets 403 on POST /api/news
P0: Viewer gets 403 on POST /api/events
P0: Viewer gets 403 on POST /api/albums
P0: Viewer gets 403 on POST /api/employees
P0: Viewer gets 403 on PUT /api/settings/hero
P0: Viewer gets 403 on PUT /api/settings/ticker
P0: Admin can POST /api/news
P0: Admin can POST /api/employees
P0: Admin can POST /api/albums
P0: Admin can PUT /api/settings/hero
P0: Admin can PUT /api/settings/ticker
P0: Admin can create editor with specific permissions (['news','events'])
P0: Admin can create viewer with specific permissions (['gallery'])
P1: Admin sidebar shows ALL 11 sections
P1: User Management page shows role badges (Admin/Editor/Viewer) with icons
P1: Permissions grid visible in Add User dialog when role=editor
P1: All 8 CMS section checkboxes visible (news,events,gallery,employees,pages,menus,hero,ticker)
P1: Permissions grid hidden when role=admin, info message shown
P1: Permissions grid visible when role=viewer with 'view only' label
P1: Editor sidebar shows only Dashboard, News, Events (matching permissions)
P1: Editor sidebar hides Activity Log, User Management, Hero, Ticker, Gallery, Employees, Pages, Menus
P1: Viewer sidebar shows only Dashboard, Gallery (matching permissions)
P1: Viewer sidebar hides all other sections
seed_data_creation: Used existing test users created by main agent
context_for_next_testing_agent: Granular RBAC feature is fully functional. Backend permissions are enforced via require_permission(section) dependency in auth.py. Frontend sidebar filtering works correctly based on user.permissions array. Test credentials available above. Backend test file at /app/backend/tests/test_permissions.py covers all permission scenarios.
